Anthrax.first_aid: 🚨 Anthrax is a serious infectious disease. DO NOT TOUCH the animal or its carcass. Contact a veterinarian immediately. This disease can spread to humans.
action_plan: Isolate the infected animal immediately from the herd.
Stop all movements of animals and people from the farm.
Burn or deeply bury any carcasses with lime as per local regulations.
Contact the local district veterinary officer immediately.
Vaccinate all healthy animals in the surrounding area.

Foot_and_Mouth_Disease.first_aid: Wash the animal's mouth and feet with a mild antiseptic solution like Potassium Permanganate (1:1000). Keep the animal in a soft, dry place.
action_plan: Isolate infected animals from the rest of the herd.
Apply boroglycerine or honey to mouth lesions.
Treat foot lesions with an antibiotic spray or cream to prevent secondary infections.
Provide soft, easily digestible food and plenty of clean water.
Ensure all other animals are vaccinated with the FMD vaccine.

Healthy_Cattle.first_aid: ✅ Your animal appears healthy. No immediate treatment required.
action_plan: Maintain regular vaccination schedules (FMD, Anthrax, Brucellosis).
Ensure a balanced diet with proper minerals and clean water.
Practice good hygiene in the cattle shed.
